paper_metadata.title: UL2: Unifying Language Learning Paradigms
paper_metadata.abstract: Existing pre-trained models are generally geared towards a particular class of problems. To date, there seems to be still no consensus on what the right architecture and pre-training setup should be. This paper presents a unified framework for pre-training models that are universally effective across datasets and setups. We begin by disentangling architectural archetypes with pre-training objectives -- two concepts that are commonly conflated. Next, we present a generalized & unified perspective for self-supervision in NLP and show how different pre-training objectives can be cast as one another and how interpolating between different objectives can be effective. We then propose Mixture-of-Denoisers (MoD), a pre-training objective that combines diverse pre-training paradigms together. We furthermore introduce a notion of mode switching, wherein downstream fine-tuning is associated with specific pre-training schemes. We conduct extensive ablative experiments to compare multiple pre-training objectives and find that our method pushes the Pareto-frontier by outperforming T5 & GPT-like models across multiple diverse setups. By scaling our model up to 20B parameters, we achieve SOTA performance on 50 well-established supervised finetuning based NLP tasks. Our model also achieve strong results at in-context learning, outperforming 175B GPT-3 on zero-shot SuperGLUE and tripling the performance of T5-XXL on one-shot summarization. On 0-shot MMLU, UL2 20B outperforms T0 and T5 models. UL2 20B also works well with chain-of-thought prompting and reasoning, making it an appealing choice for research into reasoning at a small to medium scale of 20B parameters. Finally, we apply FLAN instruction tuning to the UL2 20B model, achieving MMLU and Big-Bench scores competitive to FLAN-PaLM 62B. We release Flax-based T5X checkpoints for the UL2 20B & Flan-UL2 20B.

extraction_data.innovation: UL2 introduces a unified language learning paradigm through a Mixture-of-Denoisers (MoD) pretraining objective, combining multiple denoising tasks (e.g., span corruption, prefix LM, and S-denoising) to enable consistent performance across diverse language tasks. This approach unifies multiple learning paradigms into a single model, outperforming both GPT-like and T5-like baselines across all tasks.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (implied from context; no specific dataset named)
extraction_data.extension: Mode switching via paradigm tokens ([R], [S], [X]) allows the model to dynamically adapt its behavior during pre-training and fine-tuning, binding downstream performance to upstream training modes.
extraction_data.research_problem: Unifying diverse language learning paradigms under a single framework to improve generalization and task transfer
extraction_data.application: Language generation, language understanding, text classification, question answering, commonsense reasoning, long-text reasoning, structured knowledge grounding, information retrieval, in-context learning, chain-of-thought prompting

extraction_data.innovation: UL2 introduces a unified pre-training framework that integrates multiple language modeling paradigms—causal language modeling, prefix language modeling, span corruption, and bidirectional denoising—into a single objective, allowing a single model to perform well across both understanding and generation tasks without task-specific heads.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text generation, supervised fine-tuning, in-context one-shot learning
extraction_data.research_problem: Unifying diverse language learning paradigms into a single, general-purpose pre-training framework
extraction_data.extension: Uses a unified pre-training objective that combines multiple language learning paradigms (e.g., causal LM, prefix LM, span corruption) into a single framework, enabling a single model to excel across diverse tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: UL2 introduces a unified pre-training framework that integrates multiple language modeling paradigms—causal language modeling, prefix language modeling, span corruption, and bidirectional denoising—into a single objective, allowing a single model to excel across both understanding and generation tasks without task-specific heads.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text generation, supervised fine-tuning, in-context one-shot learning
extraction_data.research_problem: Unifying diverse language learning paradigms into a single, general-purpose pre-training framework
extraction_data.extension: Employs a unified pre-training objective that supports both encoder-decoder and decoder-only architectures, enabling consistent performance across diverse tasks with a single training setup.
